title: Yellow Discharge from Cat Vulva: Is It an Emergency?
meta_desc: Thick yellow discharge from a cat's vulva is a serious medical emergency. Learn about pyometra and why you must see a vet immediately.
content_html:
What is This Discharge?

Thick, opaque, yellow, or foul-smelling discharge from a cat's vulva is most commonly a sign of a serious infection. The most frequent cause is pyometra, a life-threatening infection of the uterus where pus accumulates inside the organ. It may also indicate severe vaginitis or a uterine stump infection if the cat has been spayed. Regardless of the exact cause, yellow discharge indicates the presence of white blood cells and bacteria, requiring professional intervention.

Urgency Level: High

Yes, this is an absolute medical emergency. If your cat is showing these symptoms, you should contact an emergency veterinarian immediately. Pyometra can lead to sepsis, organ failure, or a ruptured uterus within hours. Do not wait for an appointment or observe her overnight; immediate stabilization and likely surgery are necessary to save her life.

Why Taking a Photo Helps Triage

When you call or arrive at the clinic, showing the staff a clear photo of the discharge can be incredibly helpful. It allows the triage team to immediately assess the color, consistency, and volume of the fluid. This visual evidence helps them differentiate between normal post-heat spotting and an emergency infection, ensuring your cat receives the fastest possible care upon arrival.

faqs.question: Can a spayed cat have yellow discharge?
faqs.answer: Yes, although it is less common. It could be 'stump pyometra' if any uterine tissue was left behind, or a severe urinary tract or vaginal infection.

faqs.question: Are there other symptoms of pyometra to look for?
faqs.answer: Yes, common signs include lethargy, increased thirst, frequent urination, loss of appetite, and a bloated abdomen.

faqs.question: Is pyometra fatal if left untreated?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pyometra is almost always fatal without surgical intervention (emergency spay) or intensive antibiotic and hormonal therapy.
